transcript.text: The Koi, a monometal organic yoyo from YoYoFriends, weighting at 65.8g, the diameter of 56mm and
the width of 44mm. It is made out of 768 aluminum which gives it a pleasant premium feeling
in the hands and on the string. Speaking of strings, the one that came with the yoyo
left me quite disappointed actually, since it was almost impossible to bind with it.
Changing the string to a fatter and rougher one solved this issue. The yoyo came with
a 90mm standard pass and center track bearing. This yoyo may look on the heavy side but
it actually doesn't play heavy at all. Au contraire, it plays really fast and floaty
considering this to be an organic throw. Grind with this yoyo are super nice, the
finish is great for them. You can also thumb grind at a tilt.
Finger spins with this yoyo are awesome, despite the center knob.
In fact, the knob itself adds stability to your finger.
Rejections are good and I had no problem landing my hardest ones.
Reasons with this yoyo are amazing, this is thanks to its high wall design.
Stalls are also great and since it's so stable on the stall,
you can also land kickflips and other 0-8 tricks.
The overall stability is impressive despite its o-shape.
I had literally no issue landing my longer and tacky combos.
The weight distribution is perfectly balanced between rim weight and center weight.
In conclusion, this is a fun yoyo with a plus given by its power factor.
Personally, one of my top 3 favorite throws. At a price point of 40 euros, it's a must have.
I am DiegoB, thanks for having spent some time with me today. So long.
